strengths: Hands-on distributed-systems depth: production microservices handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, including low-latency async I/O and real-time metadata pipelines.
Early agentic-AI exposure at re:cinq including MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows, directly relevant to the role's architecture mandate.
Consistent event-driven design practice across multiple roles (GISCollective platform, Optilyz pricing, Dunnhumby data integration), matching the job's architectural preferences.
concerns: No executive or people-leadership experience: the entire career is individual-contributor and staff-level; there is no evidence of managing teams, scaling an engineering org, or reporting to a board.
Agentic-AI experience is shallow and very recent (April 2025–present), described as tooling support rather than architecting or operating production multi-agent systems at commercial scale.
No demonstrated fintech-infrastructure depth: credit systems, payment flows, real-time credit-risk modeling, and compliance across jurisdictions are absent from the resume.
response.justification: Bogdan brings solid distributed-systems engineering and a genuine, if nascent, foothold in agentic-AI tooling, but the role is a C-suite position requiring demonstrated org-scaling, board communication, and production multi-agent systems ownership — none of which appear in his history. His career is that of a highly skilled senior/staff individual contributor, not a technical executive, and the fintech-infrastructure domain knowledge the role explicitly demands is largely absent. The technical foundations are a partial match; the leadership and domain prerequisites are not.
